---
title: "GIC"
format: 
 html:
  theme: cosmo
  df-print: kable
execute:
 echo: false
---

# Suivi de mes placements
. liste manuelle des differents placements



```{python}
import polars as pl
from pathlib import Path
import datetime
from IPython.display import display, HTML

def show(df):
    display(HTML(df.to_pandas().to_html(index=False, classes="table table-striped table-hover table-sm")))

COLUMN_NAMES = ["amount", "provider", "rate", "date", "compte", "owner","type"]

MONTH_MAP = {
    "JAN": "01", "FEB": "02", "MAR": "03", "APR": "04",
    "MAY": "05", "JUN": "06", "JUL": "07", "AUG": "08",
    "SEP": "09", "OCT": "10", "NOV": "11", "DEC": "12"
}

INPUT_PATH = "data/gic_bronze_manual.csv"
OUTPUT_PATH = "data/gic_bronze.csv"

```

## Functions utiles
### Parse date
```{python}
def parse_date(val: str) -> datetime.date:
    val = val.strip()
    day = val[:2]
    month = MONTH_MAP[val[2:5].upper()]
    year = "20" + val[5:]
    return datetime.date.fromisoformat(f"{year}-{month}-{day}")
```



```{python}
def load_csv(path: str | Path) -> pl.DataFrame:
    df = pl.read_csv(
        path,
        has_header=False,
        new_columns=COLUMN_NAMES,
        infer_schema=False,
    )

    df = df.with_columns(pl.all().str.strip_chars()) #strip blank spaces


    df = df.with_columns([
        pl.col("amount").str.strip_chars().cast(pl.Float64),
        pl.col("rate")
          .str.strip_chars()
          .str.replace("%", "")
          .str.strip_chars()
          .cast(pl.Float64)
          .truediv(100)
          .round(10), #digits apparaissaient

    ])

    df = df.with_columns(
        pl.col("date").map_elements(parse_date, return_dtype=pl.Date)
    )
    df = df.sort("date")


    return df
```


### Load 
```{python}
df = load_csv(INPUT_PATH)
df.write_csv(OUTPUT_PATH)

```


## Find the issues
```{python}
#| output: asis
def summarize_issues(df: pl.DataFrame) -> pl.DataFrame:
    return (
        df.group_by(["provider", "owner"])
          .agg(pl.col("amount").sum().alias("total_amount"))
          .with_columns(
              (pl.col("total_amount")>100000).alias("Above_100K")
          )
          .sort("total_amount", descending=True)
    )

show(summarize_issues(df))
summarize_issues(df).write_csv("data/lookup_gic.csv")

```

## Keep track of the timing


```{python}
def summarize_timing(df: pl.DataFrame) -> pl.DataFrame:
    return (
        df.with_columns(
            pl.col("date").dt.strftime("%Y-%m").alias("year_month")
        )
        .group_by(["year_month","type"])
        .agg(pl.col("amount").sum().alias("total_amount"))
        .sort(["year_month","type"])
    )
show(summarize_timing(df))
summarize_timing(df).write_csv("data/timing_gic.csv")


```

## Summary


```{python}
#| output: asis
TYPE_ORDER = ["TOTAL", "NR", "RRSP"]

def compute_summary(df: pl.DataFrame) -> pl.DataFrame:

    summary = (
        df.with_columns(
            (pl.col("amount") * pl.col("rate")).alias("interest")
        )
        .group_by("type")
        .agg(
            pl.col("amount").sum().alias("sum_total"),
            pl.col("interest").sum().alias("sum_interest")
        )
        .pipe(lambda df: pl.concat([
            pl.DataFrame({
                "type": ["TOTAL"],
                "sum_total": [df["sum_total"].sum()],
                "sum_interest": [df["sum_interest"].sum()]
            }),
            df
        ]))
        .with_columns(pl.col("type").cast(pl.Enum(TYPE_ORDER)))
        .sort("type")
    )

    return summary

show(compute_summary(df))
```



```{python}
show(compute_summary(df.filter(pl.col("owner")=="Sandrine")))
```


```{python}
result = (
    df.with_columns(
        (pl.col("amount") * pl.col("rate")).alias("interest")
    )
    .group_by(["type"])
    .agg([
        pl.col("amount").sum().alias("sum_total"),
        pl.col("interest").sum().alias("sum_interest")
    ])
)

total_row = pl.DataFrame({
    "type": ["TOTAL"],
    "sum_total": [result["sum_total"].sum()],
    "sum_interest": [result["sum_interest"].sum()]
})

result = pl.concat([result, total_row])
show(result)
```
